---
title: "Vessel registry - EDA"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
v <- 
  readxl::read_excel("data-raw/HREIDAR_Islensk_skip.xlsx", guess_max = 2e5) |> 
  janitor::clean_names() |> 
  select(year = ar,
         vid = nr,
         vessel = nafn,
         uid = stadur,
         uno = numer,
         year_construction = ar_smidad,
         brv_old = brl_gomul,
         brv_new = brl_ny,
         brt = bruttotonn_maeld,
         kw0 = kw_maelt,
         hp = hp_maelt,
         length = lengd_skrad,
         loa = mesta_lengd,
         harbour = heimahofn,
         fate = endalok,
         everything())
```

## Data pattern

* Looks like for lower vessels numbers we have not the full "annual" history back in time
* For some vessels with higher numbers than ~1000 there is a general tendency that first year of entry is a function of veesel number
  * I guess this is because when a new vessel comes in it get the next available number
  * There are however some exceptions. There may be legitimate reason for this or it could indicate data entry error.

```{r, fig.height = 15, fig.width = 9}
v |> 
  filter(between(vid, 1, 3700)) |> 
  filter(between(year, 1900, 2050)) |> 
  ggplot() +
  theme_grey(base_size = 14) +
  geom_tile(aes(year, vid)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Vessel id") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1900, 2050, by = 20)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 10000, by = 100))
```

Let's calculate the number of vessels by uid (staður):

```{r}
v |> 
  filter(between(vid, 1000, 3700),
         between(year, 1963, 2025)) |> 
  count(year, uid) |> 
  ggplot(aes(year, n)) +
  geom_point() +
  facet_wrap(~ uid,
             scales = "free_y")
```

## Vessel history

* For now just looking at change in name and uid
* Could easily be expanded to include more variables (loa, brt, ...)

```{r}
# Get unique history
h <- 
  v |> 
  filter(between(vid, 1, 9995)) |> 
  filter(between(year, 1900, 2050)) |>
  select(year:fate) |> 
  rename(brv = brv_new,
         kw = kw0) |> 
  glimpse()
  arrange(vid, year) |> 
  group_by(vid) |> 
  mutate(.rid = 1:n(),
         .last = case_when(.rid == max(.rid) ~ TRUE,
                           .default = FALSE),
         v = dplyr::consecutive_id(vessel, uid, uno, .last),
         .before = vid) |> 
  # put vessel fate on last record
  fill(fate) |> 
  mutate(fate = case_when(.rid == max(.rid) ~ fate,
                          .default = NA)) |> 
  ungroup() |> 
  distinct(vid, vessel, uid, uno, .last, .keep_all = TRUE) |> 
  select(-.last)
h
```

List of vessels with no recorded fate:

```{r}
h |> 
  group_by(vid) |> 
  filter(.rid == max(.rid),
         is.na(fate))
```

... not bad
